page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc5.3xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 12 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 12 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6149 processor operating at a base frequency of 3.1 GHz. The instance is configured with 24.0 GB of system memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying on external storage solutions. Network performance is anchored by a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate a distinct performance profile. Single-core performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by bottom-quartile scores in stress-ng single-core, Geekbench single-core, and single-threaded compression tests. However, the 12 physical cores deliver average multi-core performance, with middle-tier results in stress-ng multi-core, Passmark CPU, and multi-threaded decompression. Memory bandwidth tests show average throughput for both cached and uncached operations, although the overall Passmark memory score falls into the bottom 25%.

This instance is suited for multi-threaded workloads that benefit from dedicated CPU allocation but do not require high memory-to-CPU ratios or local storage. Appropriate workloads include static web serving, software compilation, and database operations, which perform in the average tier. It is less suitable for memory-intensive applications or single-threaded tasks due to its lower single-core efficiency and 2.0 GB per core memory limitation. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the instance offers a balanced compute-to-memory profile for users requiring dedicated Intel Xeon cores without the overhead of bundled storage.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc5.3xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 12 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6149 processor running at 3.1 GHz. It is equipped with 24.0 GB of RAM, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and contains no local storage or GPU accelerators. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show a contrast between weak single-core execution and average multi-core capabilities, with middle-tier results in multi-threaded decompression, software compilation, and static web serving. This hardware profile makes the instance suitable for dedicated multi-threaded workloads, such as web serving and moderate database operations, where high memory density is not required. It offers a cost-effective option for compute-centric tasks that do not depend on local NVMe storage.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc5.3xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 12 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 24.0 GB of RAM, and 2 Gbps network bandwidth, offering average multi-core performance for web serving and compilation.

tagline: A dedicated compute-optimized instance featuring twelve Intel Xeon cores and a balanced memory profile for multi-threaded workloads without local storage.

24.0 GB of system memory with a 2.0 GB per core ratio
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Average multi-core and software compilation performance
Best suited for static web serving and multi-threaded processing
